Welcome to my Professional Portfolio
AIML-500 · Graduate Portfolio · 2025

Greetings and welcome to my portfolio.

Thank you for visiting. I'm Arvinnd K, and here you'll find a curated collection of work from my graduate studies in AI/ML Integration Leadership (AIML-500) — a course that bridges the technical world of artificial intelligence with the human dimensions of change management and responsible leadership.

This portfolio reflects my journey exploring how AI tools work, how to lead their adoption effectively, and how to design them with empathy and ethics at the centre.

🎓
AIML-500
AI/ML Integration Leadership — a graduate course covering change management, ethical AI, and hands-on AI tool development.
🤝
Design Thinking
Every artifact was built using the five-stage Design Thinking framework — Empathy, Define, Ideate, Prototype, and Test.
🤖
AI-Powered Work
Artifacts include a working AI chatbot powered by Google Gemini and a professional AI history timeline presentation.

Artifacts
AIML-500 · Arvinnd K · 2025
Artifact 01
PowerPoint Presentation

AI/ML History Visual Timeline

A professionally designed 5-slide PowerPoint presentation tracing the complete history of Artificial Intelligence — from Alan Turing's foundational 1950 paper through to the rise of ChatGPT and large language models in 2022. Covers the Dartmouth Conference, both AI Winters, the Deep Learning Revolution, and the Transformer architecture.

Slide Preview
1
Slide 1 · Title
The History of Artificial Intelligence
From the Turing Test to the Age of Generative AI · 1950s → Present
2
Slide 2 · Historical Context · 1950s – 1970s
The Foundations of AI
Turing Test (1950), Dartmouth Conference (1956), ELIZA Chatbot (1966), Shakey the Robot (1969)
3
Slide 3 · AI Winters
When the Promise Froze
First Winter (1969–1980): Lighthill Report, DARPA cuts · Second Winter (1987–1993): Expert systems collapse
4
Slide 4 · Recent Successes · 2006 – Present
The Deep Learning Revolution
Deep Learning (2006) → AlexNet (2012) → AlphaGo (2016) → Transformers (2017) → ChatGPT (2022)
5
Slide 5 · Master View
Complete AI Timeline
All 9 milestones from 1950 to Present with colour-coded legend
5 Slides1950 → 2022 AI WintersDeep Learning TransformersChatGPT
🎯 Objective
To document the complete history of AI across five structured slides — from Turing's 1950 paper through to the Generative AI era — providing visual context for how the field evolved, collapsed, and transformed over seven decades.
⚙️ Process
Content was researched and structured chronologically across four thematic sections: Foundations (1950s–1970s), AI Winters (1969–1993), Deep Learning Revolution (2006–Present), and a master Complete Timeline slide.
🛠️ Tools
Microsoft PowerPoint (slide design & layout), Claude AI (research, content structuring & milestone verification), GenAI tools (fact-checking key dates and events).
💡 Value Proposition
Demonstrates the ability to translate complex, decades-spanning technical history into a clear professional narrative. Understanding AI's cycles of hype and disillusionment is essential context for any leader navigating today's generative AI wave.
Artifact 02
AI Chatbot Application

Change & AI Leadership Coach

A fully functional AI coaching chatbot built from scratch using the Google Gemini API and the five-stage Design Thinking methodology. The bot acts as a Socratic coaching assistant — asking 1–2 clarifying questions before guiding users through ADKAR, Kotter's 8-Step Model, and CCL's 9 Leadership Competencies. Built as a single HTML file that opens in any browser instantly.

Live Preview
🤝
Change & AI Leadership Coach
AIML-500 · Powered by Google Gemini
Hi! I\'m your Change & AI Leadership Coach. Tell me your challenge or choose a scenario:
Team resisting AIAI bias concernBoard presentation
My team won't adopt our new AI tool. What should I do?
I understand — resistance is very common. Before exploring solutions, two quick questions:

1. What specific concerns has your team voiced?
2. Has leadership communicated the why behind this change?
Google Gemini APIDesign Thinking ADKAR FrameworkKotter 8-Step Responsible AIFree · 1,500/day Single HTML File
🎯 Objective
To design and build a functional AI coaching assistant that helps AIML-500 students apply change management frameworks to real AI adoption challenges using Socratic questioning.
⚙️ Process
All five Design Thinking stages applied: Empathy (student pain points), Define (problem statement), Ideate (three concepts evaluated), Prototype (HTML + Gemini API), Test (three scenario walkthroughs — resistance, bias, board communication).
🛠️ Tools
Google Gemini API (gemini-2.5-flash-lite), HTML/CSS/JavaScript, Google AI Studio (API key management), Claude AI (system prompt design and iterative debugging).
💡 Value Proposition
Demonstrates end-to-end AI product development — from user research to a deployed working application. Shows that AI leadership means building responsibly. Free to use, no server needed, accessible to anyone with a Google account.
📋 Professional Assessment

The self-assessment I completed in this workshop was a step that felt uncomfortable at first — yet it was one of the most clarifying exercises of the course. Honestly rating my own readiness across change leadership and AI integration competencies forced me to slow down and distinguish between what I understood conceptually and what I could actually do in practice. That distinction matters more than most professionals acknowledge.

What artifact could I make of this experience? I described my self-assessment to Claude and asked for ideas. The recommendation was to let this chatbot serve as the artifact — because a working coaching tool that guides others through change management challenges is itself a demonstration of growth. It shows not just that I learned the frameworks, but that I could apply them, build with them, and share them in a form that others can use.

How can I lead as a learner with my audience? By being transparent about the process, not just the outcome. This chatbot went through multiple failed API models, quota errors, and design iterations before it worked. Sharing that journey — the uncertainty, the pivots, the problem-solving — signals to my audience that competence in AI/ML is not about getting it right the first time. It is about knowing how to keep going when you do not.
